title: Spicy Smoky Uruguayan Treacle Grill
description: A nostalgic Uruguayan dish featuring treacle and a shio-koji cure, perfect for a robust outdoor grill experience.

ingredients.name: Biona Treacle
ingredients.notes: Choose a high-quality, pure treacle for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Grass-Fed Beef Short Ribs
ingredients.notes: Cut the short ribs into 1-inch thick slices for even cooking.

ingredients.name: Shio-Koji Cure
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, this Japanese-inspired cure adds a unique depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Smoked Paprika
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality smoked paprika for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Uruguayan Chimichurri
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, this tangy herby sauce is a staple in Uruguayan cuisine.

ingredients.name: Fresh Oregano
ingredients.notes: Choose fresh oregano for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced garlic adds a pungent flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Sea Salt
ingredients.notes: Use flaky sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Coarse Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper adds depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Uruguayan Chorizo
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, this spicy sausage adds a rich flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Poblano Pepper
ingredients.notes: Roasted poblano peppers add a deep, smoky flavor to the dish.

instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat (400°F / 200°C).
In a small bowl, mix together the shio-koji cure, smoked paprika, and a pinch of sea salt for the rub.
Season the beef short ribs with the rub, making sure to coat evenly.
Let the ribs sit at room temperature for 30 minutes before grilling.
Grill the ribs for 5-7 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 135°F (57°C) for medium-rare.
While the ribs are grilling, mix together the treacle, Uruguayan chimichurri, and minced garlic for the glaze.
Brush the glaze onto the ribs during the last 2 minutes of grilling.
Once the ribs are cooked, let them rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Slice the ribs against the grain and serve with sliced Uruguayan chorizo, roasted poblano peppers, and a side of Uruguayan chimichurri sauce.
